Title: A WRKY transcription factor confers broad-spectrum resistance to biotic stresses and yield stability in rice
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: reinforces cell wall/sclerenchyma and contributes to resistance against insects and pathogens; also supports plant structure and transport
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf sheath vascular bundle and sclerenchyma
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: constitutively expressed in root, stem, leaf blade, leaf sheath, and panicle; high in vascular sheath and sclerenchyma cells; initially downregulated after BPH infestation then recovers
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: constitutively expressed in root, stem, leaf blade, leaf sheath, and panicle; high in vascular sheath and sclerenchyma cells; initially downregulated after BPH infestation then recovers
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor with C-terminal WRKY domain
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: activation-tagged mutant identification; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; lignin histochemical staining and quantification; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; transient luciferase assay; insect and pathogen bioassays; field agronomic evaluation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout increases lignin accumulation and leaf-sheath sclerenchyma thickness, conferring broad-spectrum resistance to planthoppers and blast while increasing tiller and spikelet number; overexpression has opposite effects
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: brown planthopper infestation; white-backed planthopper infestation; small brown planthopper infestation; Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae infection; Magnaporthe oryzae infection; field conditions including low temperature/rain during grain filling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes weak growth and major yield reduction. Knockout can show reduced seed-setting rate and 1000-grain weight under rainy/low-temperature grain-filling conditions, indicating sensitivity at grain filling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CRISPR knockout/editing of OsWRKY36 is a promising strategy to improve broad-spectrum biotic resistance while often maintaining or increasing yield, but deployment should consider possible low-temperature sensitivity during grain filling
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of Ningjing1 and OsWRKY36-KO seedlings after 12 h BPH infestation; accession NA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsWRKY36 is the central experimentally validated gene in this study. 2. It acts as a transcriptional repressor that suppresses phenylpropanoid/lignin-associated genes and yield-related genes. 3. Loss of OsWRKY36 elevates lignin and sclerenchyma, enhances resistance to multiple insects and pathogens, and increases tiller and spikelet number, though adverse weather can reduce grain filling.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although OsWRKY36 also regulates yield-related genes IPA1 and MOC2, its metabolism-relevant effect in this paper is mediated through repression of phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthesis. Effect of OsWRKY36 knockout on Xoo resistance could not be fully confirmed in the Ningjing1 background because tested strains were not virulent to that background.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in protoplast assays; native promoters used in reporter assays

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Bphs1-D background; WT backgrounds in study
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: general phenylpropanoid entry step supplying lignin branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: deaminates L-phenylalanine to produce trans-cinnamic acid, providing the entry substrate for downstream phenylpropanoid and lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme/gateway step in phenylpropanoid pathway as stated
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: strengthens cell wall and sclerenchyma, contributing to resistance against insects and pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf sheath vascular bundle and sclerenchyma
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transcript increased in OsWRKY36-KO and decreased in OsWRKY36-OE/Bphs1-D contexts; induced relevance under BPH-associated defense
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: same as applied species in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; rescue of Bphs1-D phenotype by overexpression in mutant background; qRT-PCR; Western blot for OsPAL6/GFP-OE; BPH/WBPH/SBPH and pathogen assays; lignin staining/quantification; Y1H, EMSA, ChIP-qPCR, and luciferase assays showing direct repression by OsWRKY36
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases lignin accumulation and enhances resistance to BPH, WBPH, SBPH, Xoo PXO99, and virulent M. oryzae strains; also rescues susceptibility of Bphs1-D
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: planthopper infestation; Xoo infection; M. oryzae infection
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: slight increase in sclerenchyma thickness reported; no major negative tradeoff explicitly reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering broad-spectrum resistance via enhanced lignin/phenylpropanoid flux; can be stacked with upstream regulator editing such as OsWRKY36 knockout
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: identified among upregulated genes in RNA-seq of OsWRKY36-KO after BPH infestation; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPAL6 is a direct repression target of OsWRKY36. 2. Increasing OsPAL6 expression elevates lignin accumulation and improves resistance to multiple insects and pathogens. 3. OsPAL6 overexpression restores the defense defects of the OsWRKY36 activation-tag mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains W-box motifs directly bound by OsWRKY36; overexpression construct included OsPAL6/GFP in some assays.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCAMBIA1390-based overexpression in transgenic rice; native OsPAL6 promoter in luciferase reporter assays

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: TP309 (overexpression line from prior source); backgrounds used for expression analyses in this study
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: general phenylpropanoid entry step supplying lignin branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid for phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme/gateway step in phenylpropanoid pathway as stated
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall reinforcement and defense against insects/pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf sheath vascular bundle and sclerenchyma
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: increased in OsWRKY36-KO and decreased in OsWRKY36-OE
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: same as applied species in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; luciferase repression assays; overexpression resistance assay using previously generated lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression lines show higher resistance to BPH; gene is directly repressed by OsWRKY36 and likely contributes to lignin-mediated defense
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance broad-spectrum resistance through lignin-associated defense reinforcement
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: identified among upregulated genes in RNA-seq of OsWRKY36-KO after BPH infestation; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPAL1 is a direct transcriptional target repressed by OsWRKY36. 2. Its expression rises in OsWRKY36 knockout plants. 3. Overexpression increases BPH resistance, supporting a positive role in lignin-associated defense.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Overexpression material was obtained from previous work and used here mainly to test BPH resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native OsPAL1 promoter in luciferase reporter assays

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY family with C-terminal WRKY domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: constitutively expressed in root, stem, leaf blade, leaf sheath, and panicle; highly expressed in vascular sheath and sclerenchyma cells; initially downregulated after BPH infestation then gradually recovers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: constitutively expressed in root, stem, leaf blade, leaf sheath, and panicle; highly expressed in vascular sheath and sclerenchyma cells; initially downregulated after BPH infestation then gradually recovers
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: BPH infestation downregulates expression initially; broader biotic stress context inferred from insect/pathogen challenge
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: direct transcriptional repression through binding W-box motifs in target promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses OsPAL6 and OsPAL1 to reduce lignin biosynthesis; represses IPA1 and MOC2 to reduce spikelet number per panicle and tiller number
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: phenylpropanoid pathway/lignin biosynthesis and associated defense-related cell wall reinforcement
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf sheath vascular bundle and sclerenchyma
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: strengthens secondary cell wall for defense and plant structural integrity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major negative switch controlling lignin accumulation via direct repression of PAL genes; knockout causes strong derepression and broad-spectrum resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: activation-tagged mutant, CRISPR knockout, overexpression, RNA-seq, qRT-PCR, Y1H, EMSA, ChIP-qPCR, dual-luciferase assay, lignin assays, insect/pathogen bioassays, field yield analyses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OsWRKY36 increases lignin accumulation and sclerenchyma thickness, enhancing resistance to BPH/WBPH/SBPH and blast, and elevates tiller/spikelet numbers; overexpression reduces resistance and yield
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BPH infestation; WBPH infestation; SBPH infestation; Xoo infection; M. oryzae infection; field low temperature/rain during grain filling affected agronomic performance
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes weak growth and large yield penalties; knockout may suffer lower seed setting and 1000-grain weight under low-temperature/rainy grain-filling conditions
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A high-value editing target for breeding rice with broad-spectrum biotic resistance and generally stable yield; should be tested across environments due to possible grain-filling sensitivity
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of Ningjing1 vs OsWRKY36-KO after 12 h BPH infestation; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsWRKY36 is a nuclear WRKY repressor identified from an activation-tagged mutant. 2. It directly binds W-box motifs in OsPAL6/OsPAL1 promoters to repress phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthesis, thereby lowering defense. 3. It also represses IPA1 and MOC2, linking defense regulation with tiller and panicle traits. 4. Knocking it out provides broad-spectrum resistance with favorable yield architecture in most tested years.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper frames OsWRKY36 as a 'knob gene' balancing defense and yield. Xoo resistance of knockout plants could not be conclusively tested in the Ningjing1 background because available strains were avirulent to that background.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transient assays; overexpression constructs in transgenic rice used constitutive expression
